finding: Configuration files are duplicated across two directory trees: .work/config/ and skill/hedl/work-state/config/.
detail: Both trees carry identical copies. install.py should project from the skill template to .work/ on installation rather than maintaining two independent committed sets.
recommendation: Treat skill/hedl/work-state/config/*.json as the source; have install.py copy/symlink into .work/ on tier installation. Reconsider whether .work/config/ should be committed at all in the self-hosted repo.

finding: Work state is duplicated: .work/ and skill/hedl/work-state/ both contain context.json, phases/, session.json, work.json.
detail: Two parallel trees. If the skill copy is a template it should be marked as such and copied once on install; if .work/ is canonical the skill copy should not exist.
recommendation: Decide and document: template-only (read-only, copied by install.py) or delete the skill copy. Whichever survives, the other goes.

finding: review-library.md prompts overlap with core agents (ambiguity-hunter vs scope-auditor; contradiction-finder vs historian).
detail: Library size and core-agent remit drift toward duplication.
recommendation: Run agent-evaluator over the library; consolidate or remove prompts with >40% overlap with a core agent. Document acceptance criteria for adding a library entry.

finding: skill/hedl/SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md restate the same principles, routing rules, and vocabulary.
evidence: skill/hedl/SKILL.md vs CLAUDE.md (principles, blast-radius vocab, forbidden patterns)
detail: Principle changes require updating both. Maintenance burden grows with each new principle.
recommendation: Pick one source-of-truth (SKILL.md for distribution; CLAUDE.md for project-local layering) and reference from the other by file:section.

finding: budget_manager.py exposes reduced_at/minimal_at/deferred_at as independent config values when they are linear interpolations of session_budget.
detail: If session_budget changes the three thresholds will silently drift out of proportion. Treating them as independent invites manual drift.
recommendation: Either derive them algorithmically from session_budget or remove the 'tunable' framing and mark them as derived constants.

finding: check_pr_template.py extracts section_content() and is_placeholder() helpers that are tiny wrappers used in only one or two sites.
detail: Reduces local readability without meaningful reuse.
recommendation: Inline both into the check functions; the script grows ~15 lines and each check becomes self-contained.

finding: budget_manager.py's _locked() context manager is a single-implementation wrapper around fcntl.flock().
detail: Not a true abstraction; one impl, one caller path. Keeping it is fine as named documentation but it should not be described as an interface.
recommendation: Keep but document as a named helper, not an extension point.
